message.content:
{"analysis":"I can see the full password fragments now! Looking at the hex dump around offset 3145716, I see `K7VB3BV4WW54` (just before the central directory PK header). Combined with the first part `8XDP5Q2RT9Z`, this likely gives us the full password. But I need to verify. Let me also note that the file data seems to be split: `PASSWORD=8XDP5Q2RT9Z` in one part and `K7VB3BV4WW54` in another. Let me find the EOCD to properly extract the zip, and also search for the complete password pattern.","plan":"1. Find the end-of-central-directory (PK 05 06) to reconstruct the zip
2. The file data is 33 bytes. Extract it properly from the zip
3. Concatenate the two fragments: 8XDP5Q2RT9Z + K7VB3BV4WW54 = 23 chars","commands":[{"keystrokes":"perl -e 'local $/; open(F,"<",$ARGV[0]); $d=<F>; while($d=~/(PK.\x05\x06)/g){ printf "EOCD at %d\n", pos($d)-4}' /app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat
","duration":1.0},{"keystrokes":"dd if=/app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat bs=1 skip=1048654 count=33 2>/dev/null | od -A d -c
","duration":1.0},{"keystrokes":"perl -e 'local $/; open(F,"<",$ARGV[0]); $d=<F>; while($d=~/(PK.\x05\x06)/g){ printf "EOCD at %d\n", pos($d)-4}' /app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat
","duration":1.0}]}
